## Apple extends chip supply deal with Broadcom through 2031, raising valuation questions

A supply commitment through 2031 is revenue visibility that few chip suppliers enjoy — but it also concentrates AVGO's design-win exposure to Apple's product cycle health. With AAPL earnings July 30, the deal extension raises the stakes for any guidance miss or iPhone demand softness.

- Apple has extended its chip supply relationship with Broadcom through 2031, per the Simply Wall St headline.
- The extension locks in AVGO as a strategic Apple supplier for multiple product cycles, reducing near-term customer concentration risk on AVGO's side.
- The headline explicitly flags valuation questions returning for AVGO — implying the deal prompts a re-rating debate rather than settling one.
- No deal size, revenue contribution, or specific chip category (Wi-Fi, custom silicon, etc.) is named in the headline.
